strategies.name: CI Iteration Depth Analysis
strategies.description: Correlate number of CI check rounds per branch with Copilot session duration and success. More rounds = more Copilot commits = deeper engagement.
strategies.finding: 5 CI rounds in 51.4min session led to success; 4 CI rounds without Copilot agent = stalled branch

strategies.name: Gate Storm Detection
strategies.description: Detect anomalous gate re-trigger events by identifying time windows with >4 concurrent gate runs for a single branch. A 'gate storm' occurs when twice the expected gate count fires in the same minute.
strategies.finding: update-safe-patch had 8 concurrent gates at 11:22 (double trigger) across 5 gate rounds total.

strategies.name: Multi-Branch Parallelism Analysis
strategies.description: Measure interference between concurrently-running Copilot branches by comparing gate concurrency levels, session overlap windows, and per-branch success rates when multiple branches are active simultaneously.
strategies.finding: Two Copilot branches ran in full overlap with 45 combined gate sessions. Both succeeded independently. No cross-branch interference detected.

strategies.name: Task Category to Copilot Assignment Correlation
strategies.description: Classify Copilot branches by task category (security patch, library version bump, code refactor, bug fix) and measure correlation with Copilot agent assignment and success rate. Analyze branch name semantics as a proxy for task type.
strategies.finding: Security/functional tasks (update-golang-org-x-vuln, rename-actionpins) get Copilot agents assigned. Pure library version bumps (update-charmbracelet-golden) run as gate-only automation without a Copilot agent. This suggests task routing logic differentiates by security impact.
